destinations.evidence: Canonical async commitment system transactions establish the stored pool metadata: the block number becomes commit_block and the async commitment supplies the precompile address/input before the original is moved into async lifecycle tracking.

Result submission for fulfilled async entries rejects missing proofs and mismatched precompile address, input, block number, or executor before storing execution_result and execution_proof on the canonical metadata.

Fulfilled settlement preparation requires execution_result and non-empty execution_proof, checks the pool metadata against AsyncJobTracker/SPC job state, and annotates the replayed original transaction with an SpcCall containing the precompile address, original input, execution result, proof, and contract commit block.

The fulfilled async payload candidate installs AsyncCallMetadata with the pool transaction hash, precompile address, original input, commit block, execution result, and execution proof before executing prepared.original_tx; settlement execution happens only after that replay.

equivalence_argument: Mapped in the port under the v2.2.0 async settlement architecture. The old AsyncExec-system-transaction wording is represented by canonical async commitment metadata plus AsyncJobTracker/SPC checks: the canonical system transaction establishes precompile address/input and commit_block, result submission can only fulfill matching metadata with a proof, settlement preparation cross-checks that metadata against contract state and annotates the original transaction, and the payload builder installs the fulfilled async metadata before replaying the original transaction.
rationale: The commit subject explicitly says async execution should use metadata from the system transaction, and the hunk extracts async_exec from metadata.system_tx, uses async_exec.commit_block in extract_fulfilled_async_metadata, and includes both execution_result and execution_proof before execution. This result keeps the candidate as a distinct refined invariant because the bounded assignment packet does not contain a newer candidate that clearly supersedes it. Implementation-detail caveat: The particular helper name and context storage mechanism are internal; a different implementation could satisfy the same behavior if the precompile still observes the same fulfilled metadata before async execution.
statement: When building an async execution transaction with an available execution result, the async execution context must set fulfilled async metadata using the pool transaction hash, precompile address, original input, commit_block from the AsyncExec system transaction, execution result, and execution proof before executing the transaction.
suggested_falsification_tests: Build an async payload where metadata.system_tx is an AsyncExec transaction with a known commit_block, input, execution_result, execution_proof, and precompile address. Execute the building path with async-context enabled and assert that the async precompile/context observes fulfilled metadata containing that commit_block and proof before execution; the test should fail if the code derives commit_block from older pool metadata, omits the proof, or waits until after execution to populate metadata.
summary: Fulfilled async payload replay installs validated metadata before executing the original transaction.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Payload-builder fulfilled async replay test asserting AsyncCallMetadata is populated before original execution.
title: Async fulfillment metadata comes from system transaction
